checkpoints.title: README exists
checkpoints.description: Every repository must have a README documenting purpose, setup, and usage.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a README.md in the repo root with sections: Purpose, Setup, Usage, Contributing.

checkpoints.title: License file exists
checkpoints.description: Repository must declare its license for legal clarity.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a LICENSE file. Use https://choosealicense.com/ to pick one.

checkpoints.title: CI pipeline configured
checkpoints.description: Repository must have a CI/CD pipeline definition for automated builds and tests.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a CI pipeline. See templates/ in readiness-as-code for GitHub Actions, Azure Pipelines, and GitLab CI examples.

checkpoints.title: Tests exist
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a tests/ directory with at least one test file.

checkpoints.title: Dependency manifest present
checkpoints.description: Repository must declare dependencies via a package manager.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a dependency manifest (requirements.txt, package.json, go.mod, etc.).

checkpoints.title: No hardcoded AWS keys
checkpoints.description: Repository must not contain hardcoded AWS access keys. This check uses a high-precision pattern (AKIA prefix) that produces virtually zero false positives.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Remove hardcoded AWS keys. Use environment variables, AWS SSO, or a secrets manager. The detected pattern (AKIA...) is a real AWS access key ID.

checkpoints.title: .gitignore configured
checkpoints.description: Repository should have a .gitignore to prevent committing build artifacts and secrets.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a .gitignore appropriate for your language. See https://github.com/github/gitignore

checkpoints.title: Security policy exists
checkpoints.description: Repository should have a SECURITY.md describing how to report vulnerabilities.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a SECURITY.md with vulnerability reporting instructions.

checkpoints.title: Health check endpoint
checkpoints.description: Web services must expose a health endpoint for liveness/readiness probes. This is a pattern-based check — if your health endpoint uses a non-standard name, add an exception.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a /health endpoint that returns HTTP 200 when the service is operational.

checkpoints.title: Dockerfile or container config
checkpoints.description: Containerized services must have a Dockerfile or deployment configuration.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a Dockerfile or deployment configuration for your service.

checkpoints.title: Monitoring dashboard configured
checkpoints.description: Service must have a production monitoring dashboard showing key metrics (request rate, error rate, latency, saturation).
checkpoints.fix_hint: Create a monitoring dashboard and attest in .readiness/external-evidence.json with the dashboard URL.

checkpoints.title: On-call / incident registration
checkpoints.description: Service must be registered in the incident management system with an on-call rotation.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Register your service in your incident management platform (PagerDuty, OpsGenie, ICM, etc.) and attest in .readiness/external-evidence.json
